response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of GISCollective, GIS work for Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems engineering at Sociomantic, where he built DLang services handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and caching on Linux. However, his hands-on C++ is brief and dated (a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project), he has no Rust, and no direct routing, navigation-guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience. His recent years are TypeScript/Node product work, so he would need to re-ramp on modern C++ despite transferable systems and geodata fundamentals.
strengths: Real geospatial domain experience: founded and maintains an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map
Low-latency distributed systems work in DLang (async I/O, fibers, caching) at millions of HTTP requests/second
Strong engineering rigor: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling author, Linux and infrastructure fluency
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No direct routing, navigation systems, or tile-generation pipeline background
Recent focus (2020-present) is TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, away from systems programming
